input.title: Healthcare Patient Portal
input.description: Healthcare Patient Portal is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Angular and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Spring Boot, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Patient Registration
requirements: Register with personal details and insurance info
Verify identity via email and phone
Upload ID documents for verification
Accept terms and privacy policy

FR-02.title: Appointment Booking
requirements: View available time slots for each doctor
Book appointments by selecting doctor, date, and time
Confirmation email and SMS sent after booking
Cancel or reschedule at least 24 hours before

FR-03.title: Medical Records Access
requirements: View complete medical history
Records include diagnoses, treatments, lab results
Records in reverse chronological order
Download records as PDF

FR-04.title: Prescription Management
requirements: View current active prescriptions
Request refills for eligible prescriptions
Doctor approves refill requests
Track refill history

FR-05.title: Secure Messaging
requirements: Send messages to assigned doctors
Support text and file attachments up to 5MB
All messages encrypted in transit and at rest
Doctors can reply from their dashboard

FR-06.title: Lab Results Viewing
requirements: View lab test results with reference ranges
Results flagged if outside normal range
Historical trend charts for repeated tests
Share results with other providers

FR-07.title: Billing and Payments
requirements: View outstanding invoices and payment history
Pay bills online via credit card or bank transfer
Download itemized billing statements
Set up payment plans for large bills

FR-08.title: Appointment Reminders
requirements: Send reminder 24 hours before appointment
Send reminder 1 hour before appointment
Reminders via email and SMS
Include appointment details and location

FR-09.title: Insurance Verification
requirements: Patients can add insurance provider details
System verifies insurance eligibility
Display coverage and copay information
Update insurance information as needed

FR-10.title: Emergency Contact Management
requirements: Add emergency contacts with name, phone, and relationship
Multiple emergency contacts allowed
Contacts accessible by medical staff in emergencies
Contacts can be updated or removed

FR-01.title: Design Patient Registration UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for patient registration. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for patient registration is complete and reviewable.
The patient registration design is responsive and accessible.
The design for patient registration aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Patient Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for patient registration. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Register with personal details and insurance info; Verify identity via email and phone; Upload ID documents for verification.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required patient registration user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for patient registration are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for patient registration is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Patient Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for patient registration. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Register with personal details and insurance info; Verify identity via email and phone; Upload ID documents for verification.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for patient registration is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for patient registration enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for patient registration is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Patient Registration
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for patient registration. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for patient registration supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for patient registration are defined correctly.
Schema changes for patient registration can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Patient Registration
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for patient registration. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for patient registration enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for patient registration are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of patient registration satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate Patient Registration with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate patient registration with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by patient registration exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for patient registration integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by patient registration are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Patient Registration Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for patient registration. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for patient registration.
Security, validation, and permission checks for patient registration are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important patient registration behaviors.
